title: Ordered-CTR CatBoost with high-order strategy categories
summary: CatBoost explicitly recommends native categorical handling and builds target statistics/combinations online, which is useful here because Driver, Race, Compound, Stint, and Year interact sparsely.
rationale: CatBoost explicitly recommends native categorical handling and builds target statistics/combinations online, which is useful here because Driver, Race, Compound, Stint, and Year interact sparsely. The current best snippets mostly hand-code numeric heuristics; a raw-cat expert may recover rare strategy patterns those heuristics miss.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost specialist on raw categoricals plus a small numeric core, emphasizing interaction-rich IDs like Driver x Race, Race x Year x Compound, Compound x Stint, and Driver x Compound x Stint. Avoid external one-hot/target encoding, generate OOF predictions with grouped folds, and stack with the main model using sigmoid if calibration is needed without changing rank.
expected_effect: Most likely gain is on rare drivers/races and non-slick regimes where manual thresholds are brittle.
risk: If folds are not grouped by race context, ordered CTRs can still learn misleading near-duplicates; isotonic post-calibration can hurt AUC by introducing ties, so prefer raw scores or sigmoid for stacking.
